title: Wingstop classic wings nutrition — 13 flavors with macros + minerals
html:
TL;DR

Wingstop Classic Wings nutrition grid for all 13 flavors: Atomic, Cajun, Garlic Parmesan, Hawaiian, Hickory Smoked BBQ, Hot Honey Rub, Lemon Pepper, Louisiana Rub, Mango Habanero, Mild, Original Hot, Plain, Spicy Korean Q. Per-wing serving sizes (~31–40g), 90–120 cal, 5–8g fat, 1.5–2g sat fat, 0g trans, 45mg cholesterol, 10g protein universal across all 13 flavors. Sodium varies wildly: 30 (Plain) → 310 (Cajun). Includes vitamin D, calcium, iron, potassium columns.

Why it's in the graph

This is the anabolic scoring gold standard in the screenshot cluster — wings are the highest-protein-per-cal item Yahir captured (10g protein / 90 cal = ~44% cal-from-protein). Wingstop is the chain where BroMenu shows positive results that the user wants to see. Compare against Little Caesars where the best item is barely 50% as anabolic.

The flavor-axis is also a UX challenge: 13 flavors of one item with identical macros except sodium → the BroMenu ranker needs to either collapse them into one ("Wings — Classic, any flavor") or surface sodium-sensitive sub-rankings. Same problem will recur on Buffalo Wild Wings, Domino's, etc.

Captured 3:10 a.m. — 1.5 hours before the Taco Bell triple-shot at 4:56 a.m. So the data-grab order in that night was: 3:10 Wingstop → 4:56 Taco Bell × 3.

What's actually on the page

  • 13 flavors × per-wing serving:
    • Atomic 1ea(38.77g) 90 cal
    • Cajun 1ea(38.86g) 90
    • Garlic Parmesan 1ea(35.65g) 120
    • Hawaiian 1ea(39.28g) 100
    • Hickory Smoked BBQ 1ea(39.78g) 100
    • Hot Honey Rub 1ea(36g) 120
    • Lemon Pepper 1ea(34.91g) 120
    • Louisiana Rub 1ea(33.99g) 110
    • Mango Habanero 1ea(39.28g) 100
    • Mild 1ea(38.04g) 120
    • Original Hot 1ea(38.29g) 90
    • Plain 1ea(30.78g) 90
    • Spicy Korean Q 1ea(39.11g) 100
  • Universal macros (all flavors): 5–8g fat, 1.5–2g sat fat, 0g trans, 45mg cholesterol, 10g protein.
  • Sodium spread: Plain 30mg → Cajun 310mg.
  • Mineral columns present: vitamin D, calcium, iron, potassium (mostly trivial, some calcium 10–16mg, potassium 80–122mg).

Loose ends

  • Boneless wings, chicken sandwiches, fries, and ranch are NOT on this screenshot — the dataset for Wingstop is incomplete after this one capture.
  • Worth confirming whether classic wing bone-in weight (~37g/wing) vs boneless (different cal/protein) should be separate menu_items rows or a variant.

Links

  • wingstop
  • brotein / bromenu / anabolic scoring
  • supabase
  • fast food nutrition dataset
  • restaurant menu scraping
